The surgeon navigates through the duodenum to identify the sphincter of Oddi. If the tumor is nearby, a sphincteroplasty is performed by suturing the sphincter to the mucosa using a 5-0 absorbable suture.
The surgeon closes the mucosal defect, while the catheter is manipulated back and forth, indicating its position relative to the defect.
